class.name: Trail3D
class.brief_description: A node to draw lines behind moving objects.
class.description: The Trail3D node is a node that draws a flat line in space, either billboarded or not. This node is targeted for visual effects. For solid lines, pipes, and tubes, please refer to CGS Nodes.

method.name: get_current_length
method.description: Returns the current length of the trail.

members.member: The color of the trail. The color is readable in the shader through the vertex color. [b]Note:[/b] This does not support HDR colors.
The color gradient over the trail length. The color is readable in the shader through the vertex color. [b]Note:[/b] This does not support HDR colors.
Toggle on and off to stop and start the trail. [b]Note:[/b] Only one active trail is supported. Toggling emitting on will cause the mesh to be cleared.
The lifetime of the trail, when in [member limit_mode] lifetime.
What limits the trail length between a given time and a given length.
The material mode for the ribbon. Use material mode custom to supply a custom material.
When in limit mode length, the maximum length of the trail.

Alignment of the trail. Possible options are [constant MESH_ALIGNMENT_LOCAL] for a 3D line, and [constant MESH_ALIGNMENT_BILLBOARD] for a billboarded line. Changing it causes the ribbon to be rebuilt.
Minimum distance before adding a new section.
Pin the UVs to their positions in space.
How to tile the UVs across the length of the ribbon.
UV multiplier across the length of the ribbon.
Width across the length of the ribbon.

constants.constant: Align the trail normal to the local Y axis of the ribbon node.
Align the trail to the camera, causing each section to billboard.
Assign UVs from 0 to 1 across the length of the ribbon.
Assign Uvs from 0 to current length, across the length of the ribbon.
Limit the trail length in time.
Limit the trail length in size.
Maximum value for enum [enum LimitMode]. Relevant only when in trail mode.
Assign a built-in mix material to the trail.
Assign a built-in additive material to the trail.
Assign a custom material to the trail via [member material].
